companies.notes: Dec 2025 tender at $800B. Earlier Dec 2024 tender was ~$350B. Represents ~85% of total space sector value.
SPAC merger Aug 2021 at ~$4.1B EV. Current market cap ~$37B.
SPAC merger Dec 2021 at ~$2.8B EV.
SPAC merger Apr 2021 at ~$1.8B EV. Increased 377% in 2025.
SPAC merger Aug 2021 at ~$1.6B EV. Significant decline.
SPAC merger Sep 2021 at ~$1.5B EV.
SPAC merger Sep 2021 at ~$615M EV. Rollup strategy (Made In Space, etc.).
First private lunar lander (Feb 2024). Acquired Lanteris for $800M.

capital_invested.notes: Complex history - original Iridium bankrupted 1999, new company emerged.

companies.notes: Legacy satcom operator. Iridium NEXT constellation complete.
Apple partnership for iPhone satellite SOS boosted value.
Acquired Inmarsat 2023. GEO broadband focus.
Bankrupt Apr 2023 after UK launch failure. Assets sold for ~$36M.
Delisted, near-failure. Multiple launch failures. Pivoting to spacecraft.
Delisted 2024. SEC fraud charges against founder (Russian national security concerns).
Acquired by Lockheed Martin Dec 2024 for ~$450M. SPAC at ~$1.58B EV.

capital_invested.notes: Formed via MDA/DigitalGlobe merger. Capital represents post-merger raises.

companies.notes: Acquired by Advent International Jan 2023 for ~$6.4B.
Acquired by L3Harris Jul 2023 for ~$4.7B.

capital_invested.notes: Was division of Ball Corp (BLL). Cannot isolate capital invested.

companies.notes: Sold to BAE Systems Feb 2024 for ~$5.6B.
Merged with ATK → Orbital ATK → Acquired by Northrop Grumman 2018 for ~$9.2B total. Orbital portion ~$5.4B.
Acquired by MDA/Maxar Oct 2017 for ~$2.4B.

companies.notes: NO RELIABLE VALUATION. Excluded from quantitative analysis per methodology. Some analysts estimate $30-40B but highly speculative.

3D-printed rockets. Eric Schmidt became CEO Mar 2025.

Commercial space station. Multiple crewed missions to ISS.
Founded by ex-SpaceX propulsion lead. Space tugs.
Fully reusable rocket. Founded by ex-Blue Origin.
Small GEO comms satellites. Andreessen-backed.
Haven-1 commercial space station. Founded by Jed McCaleb (crypto).
Space-based pharma manufacturing. First capsule returned 2024.
SAR imagery constellation.
RF geolocation from space. Defense/intel focus.
Space security. Inspection satellites.

companies.notes: Eylea, Dupixent franchise. Major biotech success story.
Cystic fibrosis franchise. ~$130B market cap.
COVID vaccine. Peak ~$140B (2021), now ~$16.5B. Massive decline from peak.
HIV/HCV franchise. Major pharma-scale biotech.
Largest independent biotech by market cap.
Dominant sequencing platform. Forced to divest Grail.
First approved CRISPR therapy (Casgevy) Dec 2023.
SPAC at ~$17.5B (Sep 2021). Massive decline. Acquired Zymergen.
AI-driven drug discovery. Nvidia partnership.
LARGEST BIOTECH M&A EVER. Acquired by BMS Nov 2019 for $74B. Revlimid.
Landmark deal. Acquired by Roche Mar 2009 for $46.8B.
Acquired by Pfizer Dec 2023 for $43B. ADC platform.
Acquired by AstraZeneca Jul 2021 for $39B. Soliris/Ultomiris.
Acquired by AbbVie May 2015 for $21B. Imbruvica.
Acquired by Gilead Sep 2020 for $21B. Trodelvy.
Acquired by Pfizer Sep 2016 for $14.3B. Xtandi.
Acquired by BMS Mar 2024 for $14B. KarXT for schizophrenia.
Acquired by BMS Nov 2020 for $13.1B. Mavacamten.
Acquired by Gilead Oct 2017 for $11.9B. Yescarta.
Acquired by Merck Apr 2023 for $10.8B.
Acquired by BMS Jan 2024 for $5.8B ($4.8B closing + $1B CVR). Krazati.
SPAC at ~$3.5B (Jun 2021). Bankruptcy Dec 2024. Major failure.
IPO at ~$3B (Apr 2021). Acquired by Ginkgo Jul 2022 for ~$300M. 90%+ value destruction.
Bankruptcy Nov 2014. Provenge approved but couldn't commercialize. Classic biotech failure.
Backed by Milner, Bezos. Cellular reprogramming for longevity.
Daphne Koller. ML + biology platform.

companies.notes: ~$1.5T market cap. Dominates energy sector value like SpaceX dominates space. 9th largest company globally.
US thin-film solar leader. IRA beneficiary.
Microinverter leader. Peak >$40B, declined.
IPO Nov 2021 at $78B. Major decline. Amazon investor.

Peak ~$30B (2021). Significant decline. Hydrogen ecosystem play.
AI data center power demand driver. Up 280% in 2025.
Only NRC-approved SMR. TVA 6GW deployment contract.

ICONIC CLEANTECH 1.0 FAILURE. Bankruptcy Sep 2011. $535M DOE loan guarantee. Political flashpoint.
Bankruptcy Oct 2012. $249M DOE grant. Acquired by Wanxiang (China) for $257M.

JB Straubel (Tesla co-founder). Battery recycling leader.

Longest-running private fusion company. $6B merger with Trump Media announced Dec 2025.
Sam Altman investor. Microsoft PPA for fusion power. $425M Series F Jan 2025.
Iron-air batteries for 100-hour storage. Bill Gates backed.
Enhanced geothermal using horizontal drilling tech from oil/gas.
